title: Setting owner and member roles in a Team
what_it_is: Every person in a Teams team has a role — either Owner or Member. The Owner can change the team's settings and manage who's in it; a Member can take part but can't change those settings.
why_use_it: Getting roles right from the start means the people who should be able to manage the space can, and everyone else has a space that's set up consistently and can't be changed by accident.
how_to: Open Teams and select the team you want to check from the left-hand list.
Select the three dots (⋯) next to the team name and choose Manage team.
Select the Members tab to see everyone currently in the team, along with their role.
To change someone's role, find their name in the list, select the role shown next to it — Owner or Member — and choose the correct one from the dropdown.
To add a new person, select Add member near the top of the screen, type their name, then select Add. New people are added as Members by default.
Keep the number of Owners to those who genuinely need to manage the team's settings — every other person in the group only needs Member access to take part fully.
